trivia_bullets.text: Makindi is the name of an ancient Kor empire whose capital city stood in Ondu, Zendikar's southwestern continent. Civil war destroyed the capital, leaving the Makindi Trenches — high-walled canyons hundreds of feet deep — as its geological scar. The Sliderunner's name places it in this ruined imperial geography.
sources: MTG Wiki: Ondu, via WebSearch snippet — mtg.fandom.com/wiki/Ondu

trivia_bullets.text: "After a battle, it breaks the hillsides into manageable pieces to prepare for next time." The beast treats geological destruction as grooming behavior — apex fauna indifferent to the Eldrazi devastation reshaping its habitat.

A 2/1 Trampler for two mana with Landfall +1/+1. Landfall returns in BFZ as a callback to the original Zendikar block (2009-2010) where it debuted; Wizards used BFZ's Landfall reprint to signal plane-identity continuity even under Eldrazi devastation. Pauper-legal.
EDHREC rank 23,622 — niche draft pickup, not a Commander staple; superseded by higher-ceiling Landfall creatures at Commander table size.
